sourceMovie.title: The Experiment
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Experiment works as a thriller movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The thriller and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Stanford Prison Experiment
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Experiment clicked for you, The Stanford Prison Experiment (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Gaslight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Experiment clicked for you, Gaslight (1944) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Experiment?
faq.answer: Try The Stanford Prison Experiment, Gaslight, Intruders—each shares DNA with The Experiment while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Experiment?
faq.answer: The Experiment (2010) is best described as Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Experiment worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
